title: The Support Group Predator
omen: a thermostat set to 90 by hands that deny themselves

starter_premise.title: The Support Group Predator
starter_premise.premise: He infiltrates online widow communities and has done it in at least twelve groups already.
starter_premise.signature_tell: His profile photo is him with a golden retriever and it is not his dog.
starter_premise.base_premise: He attends grief groups and he is not grieving.

beat_sheet.text: The asymmetry is visible to the audience before it is legible to Claire Marlowe: He infiltrates online widow communities and has done it in at least twelve groups already. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.00.

The first wrong read happens in public, and from then on the county starts accumulating evidence in the wrong emotional direction. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Brooke Pruitt hesitates before explaining side room with a space heater and old family records. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.09. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

A clue involving heavy brass candlestick appears in scene one or two, but only later will anyone admit it mattered. Act one ends with the information gap already weaponized against the person who needs it most. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.41.

The police do not merely fail; they side with the wrong narrative and make Claire Marlowe newly dangerous on paper. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.54.

The ally is compromised, recants, disappears, or gets removed from usefulness before act three. She will not have clean backup. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.68. The ceramic owl is back on the kitchen island.

The county now treats the missing information as proof against her instead of proof of manipulation. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Wyatt Winterson hesitates before explaining dock office with a desk nobody fully claims. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.99. The ceramic owl is back on the kitchen island.

By the end of act two, survival depends on moving while still partially ignorant. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Claire Marlowe hesitates before explaining meditation annex nobody uses in winter. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.17.

The reveal confirms the audience's dread but still leaves enough missing context to wound certainty itself. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Daniel Mercer hesitates before explaining unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.37.

The timeline error, digital slip, or witness contradiction narrows the gap but never fully collapses it in time. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. A chase or confrontation near Channel 8 Night Report forces action before comprehension is complete. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Daniel Mercer hesitates before explaining unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.81. A phone vibrates from inside the wall.

Justice is partial or pyrrhic. The visible conspiracy breaks, but the informational gap stays open somewhere at the edge of the county. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Brooke Pruitt hesitates before explaining side room with a space heater and old family records. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 3.07. A basement light switches on in an empty house.

contradiction_ledger.entry: Wyatt Winterson is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Claire Marlowe says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Alder Ridge.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Wyatt?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Evelyn. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Claire. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The heavy brass candlestick is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Brooke."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
"Honey, ever since the accident, your anxiety has been so high. Did you take your pills?"
"That pool was supposed to make us look safe, and somehow it became a witness."
"He keeps calling it stress like stress knows my garage code."
"If she is the nanny, why is she wearing my robe like a legal argument?"
fake_critic_blurb: "The Support Group Predator understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "The Support Group Predator" follows Claire Marlowe, a sacred protagonist caught inside widow maybe lying maybe surviving before she understood where the gap had already opened. Wyatt Winterson collides with her life carrying portable suspicion, plausible access to offsite storage, and the kind of calm that sounds premeditated in a dim kitchen. The governing injury is simple: He infiltrates online widow communities and has done it in at least twelve groups already. The signature tell is always this: His profile photo is him with a golden retriever and it is not his dog. As a thermostat set to 90 by hands that deny themselves, what she cannot yet see becomes the syntax of the whole county, and every withheld fact keeps breathing just ahead of her. Where every secret comes home.
